name: Ko Olina Lagoon 4
tagline: Calm as a bathtub year-round — the best lagoon for families with babies and toddlers.
parking: Most public parking of the 4 lagoons — free lot
bestTime: Arrive by 8am weekends to guarantee parking; weekdays are easy
talkStory: Ko Olina means 'place of joy' in Hawaiian, and these four lagoons were man-made in the 1990s by blasting channels through the reef to create sheltered coves. Lagoon 4 is the farthest from the resort lobbies, so it draws fewer guests and more locals. All four lagoons are legally public beaches — ignore any resort signage implying otherwise. Sea turtles frequently swim into the lagoons to graze on algae, and the protected water is calm enough for infants year-round.
